Title: Transformation-based gene silencing and functional characterization of an ISC effector reveal how a powdery mildew fungus disturbs salicylic acid biosynthesis and immune response in the plant

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Hevea brasiliensis; Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana benthamiana
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Isochorismatase family; conserved ISC domain; unconventional secreted effector
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: fungal cell periphery/plasma membrane-associated; secreted; host cytosol
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: transcript induced during infection and further induced by sodium salicylate or chorismate treatment at 4, 24 and 48 hpi
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: fungal RNAi silencing; transgenic expression in Arabidopsis; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; in vitro ISC enzyme assay; DDHB measurement; LC-MS SA quantification; yeast invertase secretion assay; immunofluorescence and protoplast fractionation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes infection by decreasing host SA biosynthesis and SA-dependent defense
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EqIsc1 is the sole identified ISC homologue in Erysiphe quercicola and is required for full pathogenicity, as RNAi silencing reduced lesion area and haustorium formation. 2. EqIsc1 suppresses host immunity, reducing ROS burst and callose deposition, while its silencing elevates these defenses. 3. EqIsc1 lowers free salicylic acid levels in Hevea brasiliensis and Arabidopsis and increases DDHB production through ISC activity in vitro and in planta. 4. EqIsc1 is secreted and can translocate into the host cytosol, where it perturbs isochorismate homeostasis.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Enhancing SA biosynthesis/signaling or blocking EqIsc1 activity/translocation could improve resistance to powdery mildew; useful target for host-induced silencing or effector-informed resistance breeding
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: powdery mildew infection; exogenous sodium salicylate; chorismate; flg22; nlp20; DPI treatment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although EqIsc1 enzymatically hydrolyzes isochorismate like a pathway enzyme, in this pathosystem it is studied primarily as a secreted pathogen effector disturbing host metabolite biosynthesis rather than as an endogenous plant pathway gene.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequence of Erysiphe quercicola deposited in GenBank: GCA_003957845.1
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive, in Arabidopsis and Nicotiana benthamiana); RP60 and RP27 promoters used in fungal expression context
